arxiv.title: LLM Comparative Assessment: Zero-shot NLG Evaluation through Pairwise Comparisons using Large Language Models
arxiv.abstract: Current developments in large language models (LLMs) have enabled impressive zero-shot capabilities across various natural language tasks. arxiv.title: The SocialAI School: Insights from Developmental Psychology Towards Artificial Socio-Cultural Agents
arxiv.abstract: Developmental psychologists have long-established the importance of socio-cognitive abilities in human intelligence. These abilities enable us to enter, participate and benefit from human culture. AI research on social interactive agents mostly concerns the emergence of culture in a multi-agent sett

arxiv.title: Large Language Models as Superpositions of Cultural Perspectives
arxiv.abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are often misleadingly recognized as having a personality or a set of values. We argue that an LLM can be seen as a superposition of perspectives with different values and personality traits. LLMs exhibit context-dependent values and personality traits that change based

arxiv.title: Automated Knowledge Modeling for Cancer Clinical Practice Guidelines
arxiv.abstract: Clinical Practice Guidelines (CPGs) for cancer diseases evolve rapidly due to new evidence generated by active research. Currently, CPGs are primarily published in a document format that is ill-suited for managing this developing knowledge. A knowledge model of the guidelines document suitable for p

arxiv.title: AIOptimizer - Software performance optimisation prototype for cost minimisation
arxiv.abstract: This study presents AIOptimizer, a prototype for a cost-reduction-based software performance optimisation tool. The study focuses on the design elements of AIOptimizer, including user-friendliness, scalability, accuracy, and adaptability. To deliver efficient and user-focused performance optimisatio

arxiv.title: Transformers are Universal Predictors
arxiv.abstract: We find limits to the Transformer architecture for language modeling and show it has a universal prediction property in an information-theoretic sense. We further analyze performance in non-asymptotic data regimes to understand the role of various components of the Transformer architecture, especial

arxiv.title: Anomaly Detection in Automated Fibre Placement: Learning with Data Limitations
arxiv.abstract: Conventional defect detection systems in Automated Fibre Placement (AFP) typically rely on end-to-end supervised learning, necessitating a substantial number of labelled defective samples for effective training. However, the scarcity of such labelled data poses a challenge. To overcome this limitati

arxiv.title: Investigation of Compressor Cascade Flow Using Physics- Informed Neural Networks with Adaptive Learning Strategy
arxiv.abstract: In this study, we utilize the emerging Physics Informed Neural Networks (PINNs) approach for the first time to predict the flow field of a compressor cascade. Different from conventional training methods, a new adaptive learning strategy that mitigates gradient imbalance through incorporating adapti

arxiv.title: Handwritten and Printed Text Segmentation: A Signature Case Study
arxiv.abstract: While analyzing scanned documents, handwritten text can overlap with printed text. This overlap causes difficulties during the optical character recognition (OCR) and digitization process of documents, and subsequently, hurts downstream NLP tasks. arxiv.title: Graph Embedded Intuitionistic Fuzzy Random Vector Functional Link Neural Network for Class Imbalance Learning
arxiv.abstract: The domain of machine learning is confronted with a crucial research area known as class imbalance learning, which presents considerable hurdles in precise classification of minority classes.
